title: ABC9: Fix crash on bad box topology
body:
_What are the reasons/motivation for this change?_

Fix #5091 

_Explain how this is achieved._

Use `&dch -r`, as per https://github.com/berkeley-abc/abc/issues/432

_Make sure your change comes with tests. If not possible, share how a reviewer might evaluate it._

With Yosys 0.52, running on debian, run the example from https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/issues/5091#issuecomment-3256187150.

For a much smaller example: With 0e4bb293e, and Verific, running on ubuntu-24.04, download [bad.sv](https://gist.github.com/KrystalDelusion/51898b7ee79f75fc245ed029ab892a70) (minimized from https://github.com/maarizalmamun/hardware-img-decomp-algo), and call `yosys -p "synth_analogdevices -top project -nodsp" bad.sv`.  ABC should fail, leading to the `read_aiger` erroring.  Applying the changes from this patch (still using 0e4bb293e) will synthesize successfully.

This is a super fun one, because I can add a `hierarchy -top project` after the `read -sv` and it still errors, but a `write_rtlil` or `write_verilog` before the `synth_analogdevices` causes it to complete normally.  `dump` doesn't affect it, _unless_ I call `dump =*`, and then it completes normally.
